learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a strong general understanding of the historical and contemporary significance of law (Australian, Indigenous, international and comparative) for Indigenous peoples in Australia.
Demonstrate an in-depth understanding of some legal issues concerning Indigenous peoples.
Reflect critically on laws role in producing Indigenous peoples experiences of domination and injustice, and on laws potential role in overcoming such domination and injustice.
Undertake effective research into legal issues concerning Indigenous peoples.
Demonstrate skills in effective communication across a range of formats and audiences.

assessments.task_description: Students will be required to complete a 1 hour open-book exam with 10 minutes planning time. There will be one question for students to answer during the designated time.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be required to write a research essay worth 60% of the assessment for the course. The word limit is 3000 words (excluding references). Essays that exceed the word limit will be penalised. For each 200 words or part thereof that exceeds the word limit, three marks out of the 60 marks available for the essay will be deducted from the final grade.

Students must respond to one of the two set essay questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments must be submitted electronically in Word format via the online assessment submission link on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 11(03 Aug - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Seminars

Seminar Topics are:

  1. Introduction
  2. Legal Histories of Colonisation
  3. Constitutional Law (part 1)
  4. Constitutional Law (part 2)
  5. Rights to Land (part 1)
  6. Rights to Land (part 2)
  7. Racial Discrimination
  8. Child Welfare
  9. Criminal Law
  10. Practical Lawyering and Conclusion

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
